_comment: Real Indian government schemes, hand-curated as a seed set for the Scheme Navigator RAG index. Not exhaustive (docs/ROADMAP.md tracks growing this toward 400+) - covers the categories the four personas actually need: farm credit, gig/informal income, tax-saving, insurance, disability, and women/child savings.

schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
schemes.benefit: Short-term credit for crop production and allied activities at a subsidised interest rate (as low as 4% p.a. with prompt repayment, after interest subvention).
schemes.eligibility: Any farmer - owner-cultivator, tenant farmer, sharecropper - including those with small/marginal holdings.
schemes.description: A revolving credit line against farmland, meant to replace informal moneylenders for input costs (seed, fertiliser, labour) each sowing season. Issued by nationalised banks, regional rural banks, and cooperative banks.
schemes.apply_via: Nearest bank branch or Common Service Centre; Aadhaar and land records required.

schemes.name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
schemes.benefit: ₹6,000/year paid in three equal instalments directly to the farmer's bank account.
schemes.eligibility: All landholding farmer families, subject to exclusion criteria (institutional landholders, income-tax payers, and a few other categories).
schemes.description: Direct income support to ease cash-flow pressure between harvests, independent of any loan.
schemes.apply_via: PM-KISAN portal or Common Service Centre, needs land records and Aadhaar-linked bank account.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.benefit: Crop insurance against yield loss from natural calamities, pests, and disease, at a low farmer premium (1.5-5% of sum insured depending on the crop/season).
schemes.eligibility: Farmers growing notified crops in notified areas, both loanee (via KCC) and non-loanee farmers.
schemes.description: Protects against the season a Kisan Credit Card can't - a failed harvest, not just a cash-flow gap.
schemes.apply_via: Automatically enrolled for KCC loanee farmers unless opted out; non-loanee farmers apply via bank, CSC, or the PMFBY portal.

schemes.name: MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)
schemes.benefit: Legal guarantee of 100 days of wage employment per financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer for unskilled manual work.
schemes.eligibility: Any rural household; work is demand-driven - you register and request work.
schemes.description: A predictable income floor between agricultural seasons - the SIII (income intelligence) model treats MNREGA availability as a known seasonal pattern, not a windfall.
schemes.apply_via: Gram Panchayat job card registration.

schemes.name: Public Provident Fund (PPF)
schemes.benefit: Government-guaranteed ~7.1% annual return (rate reviewed quarterly), tax-free maturity, deduction up to ₹1.5L/year under Section 80C.
schemes.eligibility: Any resident Indian individual.
schemes.description: The lowest-risk 80C option - 15-year lock-in, zero market exposure, meant for long-term goals rather than liquidity.
schemes.apply_via: Any nationalised bank, major private bank, or post office.

schemes.name: National Pension System (NPS)
schemes.benefit: Market-linked retirement corpus; an additional ₹50,000/year deduction under Section 80CCD(1B), separate from and on top of the ₹1.5L Section 80C limit.
schemes.eligibility: Any Indian citizen aged 18-70.
schemes.description: Often the highest-value 'next' scheme for someone who has already maxed out 80C via PPF/ELSS, since the 80CCD(1B) deduction is additional, not overlapping.
schemes.apply_via: eNPS portal, or via a bank/POP-SP (Point of Presence Service Provider).

schemes.name: Equity Linked Savings Scheme (ELSS)
schemes.benefit: Same ₹1.5L Section 80C deduction as PPF, market-linked returns, shortest lock-in of any 80C instrument (3 years).
schemes.eligibility: Any resident Indian individual, via any SEBI-registered mutual fund.
schemes.description: The higher-risk, higher-liquidity alternative to PPF for the same tax deduction - suits someone who wants their 80C money accessible sooner.
schemes.apply_via: Any mutual fund AMC directly, or a broker/distributor platform.

schemes.name: Section 80DD - Disability Dependent Deduction
schemes.benefit: Flat deduction of ₹75,000/year (₹1,25,000 for severe disability, 80%+) for expenses on the medical treatment, training, and rehabilitation of a dependent with a disability, or premiums on a specified insurance scheme for their maintenance.
schemes.eligibility: A resident individual with a dependent (self, in the case of Section 80U) certified as disabled by a recognised medical authority.
schemes.description: A deduction, not a refund of actual expenses - claimable at the flat amount regardless of what was actually spent, once the disability certificate is on file.
schemes.apply_via: Claimed directly in the annual income tax return, supported by Form 10-IA / the disability certificate.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.benefit: ₹2 lakh life cover for ₹436/year (rate reviewed periodically).
schemes.eligibility: Bank/post-office account holders aged 18-50 (cover continues to 55) who give auto-debit consent.
schemes.description: The cheapest formal life cover available - relevant for a sole-earner household weighing an informal loan against basic financial protection.
schemes.apply_via: Auto-debit mandate through any bank/post-office savings account.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.benefit: ₹2 lakh accidental death/full disability cover, ₹1 lakh partial disability, for ₹20/year.
schemes.eligibility: Bank/post-office account holders aged 18-70 who give auto-debit consent.
schemes.description: Near-zero-cost accident cover - often paired with PMJJBY as the two-scheme baseline safety net.
schemes.apply_via: Auto-debit mandate through any bank/post-office savings account.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
schemes.benefit: Zero-balance savings account, a RuPay debit card with accident insurance, and eligibility for an overdraft facility once the account has an operating history.
schemes.eligibility: Any Indian citizen without an existing bank account.
schemes.description: The entry point that made 530M+ Indians bank-account holders - relevant as the on-ramp for a user with no PTP Level 1 data yet.
schemes.apply_via: Any bank branch or business correspondent, no minimum balance required.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana
schemes.benefit: Collateral-free loans up to ₹20 lakh (Shishu/Kishor/Tarun/Tarun Plus tiers) for non-farm micro and small enterprises, at bank-determined but regulated rates - not the 40-120% an unregistered app charges.
schemes.eligibility: Any non-corporate, non-farm small/micro business (including gig-economy-adjacent ventures like a small delivery fleet or a shop).
schemes.description: The formal-sector alternative to a scam loan app for someone trying to expand a small income-generating activity rather than just cover a shortfall.
schemes.apply_via: Any participating bank, NBFC, or MFI; apply via the PMMY portal or branch.

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
schemes.benefit: Guaranteed monthly pension of ₹1,000-₹5,000 from age 60, based on contribution amount and age at entry.
schemes.eligibility: Any Indian citizen aged 18-40 with a bank/post-office account; especially aimed at unorganised-sector workers with no employer pension.
schemes.description: Built for exactly the income profile SAHAJ models under SIII - irregular, no employer, no existing pension coverage.
schemes.apply_via: Any bank or post office savings account, auto-debit contribution.

schemes.name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
schemes.benefit: ~8% annual return (rate reviewed quarterly, among the highest of small-savings schemes), Section 80C deduction, tax-free maturity, for a girl child's education/marriage corpus.
schemes.eligibility: Parent/guardian of a girl child under 10, one account per girl (up to two per family).
schemes.description: Highest-yielding small-savings instrument available, specifically earmarked so the funds aren't diverted to a short-term cash crunch.
schemes.apply_via: Any bank or post office, birth certificate of the girl child required.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat - PM Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
schemes.benefit: ₹5 lakh/family/year cashless health cover at empanelled hospitals.
schemes.eligibility: Families identified via SECC 2011 deprivation/occupational criteria; state extensions cover additional categories in some states.
schemes.description: Removes the single most common trigger for a predatory loan in the personas SAHAJ is built for - an unplanned hospital bill.
schemes.apply_via: Check eligibility on the PM-JAY portal or at any empanelled hospital's Ayushman Mitra desk; Ayushman card issued via CSC.

schemes.name: Stand-Up India
schemes.benefit: Bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore for greenfield enterprises in manufacturing, services, or trading.
schemes.eligibility: At least one woman or SC/ST borrower per bank branch, for a new (not existing) enterprise.
schemes.description: Larger-ticket formal credit than MUDRA, aimed at first-time entrepreneurs from underrepresented groups.
schemes.apply_via: Any scheduled commercial bank branch, or the Stand-Up India portal.

schemes.name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
schemes.benefit: Credit-linked capital subsidy (15-35% of project cost depending on category/area) for setting up new micro-enterprises.
schemes.eligibility: Individuals 18+, project cost up to ₹50 lakh (manufacturing) or ₹20 lakh (services).
schemes.description: Subsidised startup capital for someone looking to move from gig/irregular income into owning a small fixed enterprise.
schemes.apply_via: PMEGP e-portal, implemented through KVIC, KVIB, and District Industries Centres.

schemes.name: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS)
schemes.benefit: Among the highest fixed government-backed interest rates available (reviewed quarterly), quarterly payout, Section 80C eligible up to ₹1.5L.
schemes.eligibility: Individuals 60+ (55+ for retirees under superannuation/VRS with conditions).
schemes.description: Relevant for an older dependent in a household SAHAJ is modelling, not the primary user personas directly.
schemes.apply_via: Any bank or post office.

schemes.name: Kisan Vikas Patra (KVP)
schemes.benefit: Fixed-return small-savings certificate that doubles the invested amount over a government-set maturity period (rate reviewed quarterly), no 80C benefit.
schemes.eligibility: Any resident individual (despite the name, not restricted to farmers).
schemes.description: No tax break, but simple and guaranteed - relevant as a plain savings option distinct from PPF/ELSS's 80C-linked ones.
schemes.apply_via: Any post office or authorised bank.

schemes.name: e-Shram (National Database of Unorganised Workers)
schemes.benefit: Free e-Shram card that acts as the entry point to unorganised-sector welfare schemes and accident insurance cover (₹2 lakh under PMSBY bundled in for the first year).
schemes.eligibility: Unorganised-sector workers aged 16-59, including gig and platform workers, street vendors, domestic workers, and agricultural labourers.
schemes.description: The registration layer most directly aimed at Rajesh's category of worker - a Swiggy driver has no other formal employment record without it.
schemes.apply_via: e-Shram portal or Common Service Centre, Aadhaar-linked.
